free_text: Nuclear charge distribution in proton-induced fission
of 238U
Beam intensity is 0.1 to 0.2 micro-Ampere.
- Chemical-form of target is element. 238U:99.3% and
235U:0.68% of reagent grade was purified by the
ethylether extraction, precipitated as oxalate, and
then ignited to the oxide, U3O8. Uranium targets
were prepared fro mthe fine powder of the purified
U3O8by a centrifuge technique using liquid paraffin
as adispersion medium.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness: Thickness of the uranium layer was
- Backing is aluminium.
- Backing-thickness is 6 mg/cm**2.
Institute for Nuclear Study
Calibration: Proportional counters and gamma
spectrometers were calibrated with activities of 148Pm
and 148mPm which were standardized by the 4pi beta
counting or 4pi beta-gamma coincidence measurements
[H.Baba et al., J. Appl. Radiat. Isotopes 21(1970)607,
H.Baba et al., J. Appl. Radiat. Isotopes 22(1971)141].
End-window proportional counter to measure beta
activities of samples
NaI(Tl) to measure 134Cs, 160Tb, and 148Pm
activity
Errors quoted are the mean deviations among
different runs of measurements. In cases,
however, where the mean deviation was too small
compared with the uncertainty of a single
measurement, the uncertainties estimated from
all possible errors associated with the decay
curve analysis, chemical yield determination,
counting efficiency, and so on were quoted
instead, as well as in the case of single
measurement.

Author's comment: The 5.4 day 148Pm (ground state) was
hardly determined because of its low fission yield and
small counting efficiency. The main product of 148Pm
was the isomer, 41.8 day 148Pm (meta stable), which
was determined with good accuracy.
